name: Sierra de Toxiza Loop from Mondoñedo
name_original: Sierra de Toxiza – circular desde Mondoñedo
description: This difficult 9.3-mile jogging route from Mondoñedo through Sierra de Toxiza offers 2172 feet of elevation gain and scenic mountain views.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Mondoñedo traverse a landscape characterized by lush, green hills and diverse natural features in the province of Lugo, Galicia. The region encompasses the Mondoñedo Valley, featuring old mountains and extensive forests of pine, eucalyptus, and oak. Water features such as the Masma river and its tributaries, along with waterfalls, contribute to the varied terrain. This area is part of the "Terras do Miño" biosphere reserve, offering a mix of cultivated areas, wetlands, and mountain wetlands. Best jogging routes around Mondoñedo The most popular jogging route is Mondoñedo Cathedral – Mondoñedo loop from Mondoñedo, a 9.4 miles (15.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 6 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and views of the town's historic center. Another top favourite among local runners is Sierra de Toxiza Loop from Mondoñedo, a difficult 9.3 miles (15.0 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs through forested areas and open mountain terrain. Local runners also love the A Rapadoira Beach – Coastal Walkway of Foz loop from Foz, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail leading through coastal paths and beach areas, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Jogging around Mondoñedo is defined by forested valleys, river paths, and coastal walkways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal runs to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Mondoñedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Mondoñedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Mondoñedo?
A: There are over 25 running routes around Mondoñedo, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from easy paths to more challenging mountain trails.

Q: Are there easy running trails suitable for beginners or families in Mondoñedo?
A: Yes, Mondoñedo offers several easy and moderate running routes. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the region's diverse terrain includes paths suitable for a leisurely jog. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31464427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Rapadoira Beach – Coastal Walkway of Foz loop from Foz&lt;/a&gt;, which offers coastal views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Mondoñedo's jogging routes?
A: Mondoñedo's jogging routes traverse a varied landscape. You can expect lush, green hills, extensive forests of pine, eucalyptus, and oak, and paths along rivers like the Masma. Some routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991294920/sierra-de-toxiza-circular-from-mondonedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Toxiza – circular from Mondoñedo&lt;/a&gt;, feature challenging climbs through forested areas and open mountain terrain, while others offer more gentle coastal or valley paths.

Q: Are there any running routes that feature waterfalls or other natural attractions?
A: Absolutely! The Mondoñedo region is rich in water features. You can discover stunning waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto do Coro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and others within the Area Recreativa de Brea. The path leading to Salto do Coro is particularly noted for its beautiful array of trees, especially between spring and autumn.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Mondoñedo?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Mondoñedo are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example of a challenging circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991294920/sierra-de-toxiza-circular-from-mondonedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Toxiza – circular from Mondoñedo&lt;/a&gt;, which offers rewarding views and varied terrain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I might see while running in Mondoñedo?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Mondoñedo offers cultural landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31813961" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo – Mondoñedo Cathedral loop from Mondoñedo&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the town's historic center, including the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/368178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6979461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte Vella (Old Fountain)&lt;/a&gt; or traditional &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/333437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galician hórreos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Mondoñedo?
A: The running routes in Mondoñedo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Over 800 runners have explored the varied terrain, often praising the scenic beauty of the forested valleys, river paths, and coastal walkways.

Q: Are there any running trails accessible via public transport in Mondoñedo?
A: Mondoñedo is a significant stop on the Camino del Norte, a well-established pilgrimage route. While specific public transport links to trailheads aren't detailed, the town itself is accessible, and routes starting directly from Mondoñedo, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31813961" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo – Mondoñedo Cathedral loop from Mondoñedo&lt;/a&gt;, would be easily reachable.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Mondoñedo?
A: The region's lush, green landscape suggests pleasant running conditions during spring, summer, and early autumn. The path to Salto do Coro, for instance, is noted for its beautiful trees between spring and autumn. Galicia's climate is generally mild, but checking local weather forecasts is always recommended for optimal comfort.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners in Mondoñedo?
A: Yes, Mondoñedo offers several difficult routes for experienced runners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31813961" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo – Mondoñedo Cathedral loop from Mondoñedo&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 9.4-mile trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991294920/sierra-de-toxiza-circular-from-mondonedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Toxiza – circular from Mondoñedo&lt;/a&gt;, which features challenging climbs through forested and open mountain terrain.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails around Mondoñedo?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on every trail are not provided, many natural areas in Galicia are generally dog-friendly, especially on leash. Given the region's natural beauty and extensive outdoor spaces, it's likely that many trails would accommodate well-behaved dogs. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect local signage.
tips: The Cathedral Basilica of the Virgin of the Assumption of Mondoñedo is one of the episcopal sees of the diocese of Mondoñedo-Ferrol in Galicia. The temple, in the Romanesque style with later additions and reforms, also has contributions from the Baroque and Gothic styles. Although its construction dates from the 13th century, the cathedral was not completed until the 14th century. Later, during the Middle Ages, a choir, the ambulatory and the sacristy were built, while the cloister with a square plan with semicircular arches and Doric columns dates from the 17th century.
Natural recreational space with multiple trails for activities along the Tronceda River. Don't forget to see the waterfall and the water channel.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Sierra de Toxiza – circular from Mondoñedo route?
faqs.answer: This route is officially categorized as a difficult jogging trail, demanding very good fitness, sure-footedness, sturdy shoes, and some alpine experience. It features significant elevation gain and varied terrain, including uneven paths, dirt roads, and potential muddy sections.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 15 km long with over 660 meters of elevation gain. For an experienced jogger, it typically takes around 2 hours to complete, but this can vary significantly based on fitness level and pace.

faqs.question: Where does the Sierra de Toxiza circular route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route conveniently starts from Mondoñedo, often commencing near the Plaza de la Catedral. You can find public parking within the town of Mondoñedo, making it accessible for those arriving by car.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Toxiza – circular from Mondoñedo trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions mentioned, dogs are generally welcome on this trail. Please ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other trail users, and always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog the Sierra de Toxiza circular route?
faqs.answer: The region's lush, green hills suggest it's beautiful throughout the warmer months. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, avoiding the peak heat of summer. Be prepared for varied weather conditions, especially in the mountains.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and jog the Sierra de Toxiza – circular from Mondoñedo trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse natural landscapes within the 'Terras do Miño' biosphere reserve. Expect lush green hills, ancient mountains, and extensive forests of pine, eucalyptus, and oak. The trail offers open mountain vistas, technical sections, and views of striking mountains and cliffs formed by granodioritas.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or points of interest will I encounter?
faqs.answer: The route starts from the historic town of Mondoñedo, home to the impressive Mondoñedo Cathedral. Further along the trail, you'll also pass by the scenic Tronceda River Waterfalls, offering a beautiful natural highlight.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. You will find it intersects with parts of the Camiño natural de San Rosendo (Foz - Mondoñedo), MTB San Rosendo, Northern Route (Galiza), and Mtb Northern Route (Galiza).

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this challenging jogging route?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, sturdy trail running shoes with good grip are essential. Dress in layers suitable for changing mountain weather, and bring enough water, snacks, and a map or GPS device. Poles can be helpful for the significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: Are there any public transport options to reach the trailhead in Mondoñedo?
faqs.answer: Mondoñedo is a well-established town, and while specific bus routes to the trailhead aren't detailed, regional bus services typically connect it to larger towns. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to Mondoñedo.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Due to its 'difficult' classification, significant elevation gain, and requirement for 'very good fitness' and 'sure-footedness', this specific circular route is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. There are likely easier, more family-friendly walks available closer to Mondoñedo.
